thread.section_title: Live Feed Wire - Golf News Today - EIN Presswire

author: @walbnews10
text:
ALBANY, Ga. (WALB) - As the Thanksgiving holiday approaches, local motorcycle clubs and social clubs in Albany are giving back to the community while trying to address the homeless population.
They told WALB that they realized that a lot of people in Albany are in need.
“It’s kind of like a yard sale, however, we’re not charging anybody for anything, so all of the items that we’re giving away are completely free, including the food and drink items,” said Milton Reese, co-chair for SWGA Motorcycle Coalition and Social Club.
John Bolden, president of MOB Motorcycle Club, told WALB that they got together in one of their meetings and decided that, as motorcycle clubs, they wanted to do something for the community as one big group.
The motorcycle club also holds events during different times of the year.
Around Halloween time, they hold a yearly haunted house at their clubhouse. They also help feed those who stay at the Oglethorpe Motel, which is located right next to the clubhouse.
“We eventually, in the future want to get together and put together an outside event, like a carnival for kids to just come around and we going to kind of put it together and make it something like a school giveaway. But there’ll be games and prizes as the school supplies will be the prizes for the carnival,” said Bolden
Bolden says these events help remove the negative stereotype given to motorcycle clubs by TV shows.
“Everybody has seen Sons of Anarchy and they only highlight the bad things that happen with the motorcycle clubs. But in actuality, there’s a lot more to it than what people see on TV and it gives us a bad rap. But motorcycle clubs actually do more for the community than what people think. And this is an example of what’s going on and the things that actually happen here in Albany,” he said.
Marvin Thomas Jr. with the Viper Unit Motorcycle Club told WALB that they plan to hold a Christmas party in December where club members, along with family and friends can donate toys that will be donated to an organization.
The Viper Unit Motorcycle Club will have more information about the Christmas party on their Facebook .
